title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type Recall
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for http semantics, roles, representations, and layered evidence, producing protocol evidence map. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch protocol evidence map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate a resource from the selected representation and its metadata, content, and current validity. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Client Server Roles · changed case · Review Client Server Roles
steps.instruction:
review case — A review offline design-system client request T-2 crosses a resolver, TLS connection, gateway, cache, and origin before a changed payload is rejected. Separate every role, representation, state holder, and failure layer before editing code. Competency probe: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Which action demonstrates Client Server Roles without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Client Server Roles · changed case · Review Client Server Roles.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Client Server Roles · changed case · Review Client Server Roles, keep Client Server Roles connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
